morphology: Giemsa smear; Schuffner dots; enlarged/oval infected RBCs; amoeboid trophozoites; tertian (48h) fever cycle
diseases: Benign tertian malaria; dormant liver hypnozoites cause relapse
treatment: Chloroquine (blood stage) + primaquine (kills hypnozoites)
pearl: Check G6PD before primaquine (hemolysis). Vivax needs Duffy antigen -> rare in West Africans (Duffy-negative); ovale relapses in W. Africa.

name: Babesia microti
morphology: Giemsa smear: ring forms (resemble falciparum) + pathognomonic Maltese cross tetrad; NO gametocytes; NO hemozoin pigment
diseases: Babesiosis: fever, hemolytic anemia; severe/fulminant in asplenic patients; NO periodic fever cycle
treatment: Atovaquone + azithromycin (mild); clindamycin + quinine (severe)
pearl: Ixodes scapularis tick (same as Lyme, NE USA); asplenia = severe disease; Maltese cross is the buzzword; can co-infect with Borrelia.

name: Entamoeba histolytica
morphology: Trophozoite with ingested RBCs (erythrophagocytosis); mature cyst with 4 nuclei; flask-shaped colon ulcers
diseases: Amebic dysentery (bloody diarrhea); anchovy-paste right-lobe liver abscess
treatment: Metronidazole/tinidazole + luminal agent (paromomycin or iodoquinol) to clear cysts
pearl: Fecal-oral cysts; RUQ liver abscess; serology positive. Few/no fecal leukocytes (trophozoites lyse them).

name: Giardia lamblia (duodenalis)
morphology: Pear-shaped trophozoite, 2 nuclei + 4 pairs flagella = falling-leaf motility; 'owl/old-man' face; stool antigen/ELISA
diseases: Giardiasis: fatty, foul-smelling, non-bloody steatorrhea; bloating, malabsorption
treatment: Metronidazole (or tinidazole, nitazoxanide)
pearl: Hikers/campers (mountain streams), daycare, IgA deficiency. Cysts resist chlorination. Found in duodenum.

name: Toxoplasma gondii
morphology: Tachyzoites (acute) and tissue cysts with bradyzoites (latent); ring-enhancing brain lesions on MRI; serology IgM/IgG
diseases: Congenital: chorioretinitis, hydrocephalus, intracranial calcifications. AIDS (CD4<100): brain abscess/encephalitis
treatment: Sulfadiazine + pyrimethamine (+ leucovorin); TMP-SMX for prophylaxis in HIV
pearl: Cat feces (oocysts) & undercooked meat (tissue cysts); avoid litter boxes in pregnancy. Multiple ring-enhancing lesions.

name: Cryptosporidium parvum
morphology: Acid-fast oocysts in stool; small round red oocysts on modified acid-fast (Kinyoun) stain
diseases: Severe chronic watery diarrhea in AIDS (CD4<100); mild self-limited watery diarrhea if immunocompetent
treatment: Supportive + restore immunity (ART); nitazoxanide (effective mainly in immunocompetent)
pearl: Acid-fast oocysts; chlorine-resistant -> waterborne outbreaks (Milwaukee 1993). HIV opportunist.

name: Trichomonas vaginalis
morphology: Motile pear-shaped trophozoite on wet mount (jerky/twitching motility); no cyst form; vaginal pH >4.5
diseases: Vaginitis: frothy yellow-green malodorous discharge, strawberry cervix, dysuria; men usually asymptomatic
treatment: Metronidazole or tinidazole (treat partner too)
pearl: STI, motile trophozoite on wet prep, strawberry cervix. No cyst stage -> transmitted only as trophozoite.

name: Enterobius vermicularis (pinworm)
morphology: Scotch-tape test: eggs flattened on one side (D-shaped/asymmetric); small white worms in perianal region
diseases: Perianal pruritus, especially nocturnal; common in children
treatment: Pyrantel pamoate or albendazole/mebendazole; repeat in 2 weeks; treat household
pearl: Scotch-tape test; nighttime perianal itching in kids; fecal-oral autoinfection.

name: Ancylostoma / Necator (hookworm)
morphology: Oval thin-shelled eggs in stool; larvae penetrate skin of feet; buccal teeth (Ancylostoma) / cutting plates (Necator)
diseases: Microcytic iron-deficiency anemia; pruritic feet (ground itch); Loeffler-like pneumonitis during lung migration
treatment: Albendazole or mebendazole + iron supplementation
pearl: Larvae penetrate bare feet (walking barefoot); chronic GI blood loss -> iron-deficiency anemia. (Cutaneous larva migrans is from animal Ancylostoma braziliense.)

name: Ascaris lumbricoides
morphology: Largest intestinal nematode; knobby/mammillated oval eggs in stool; lung migration (Loeffler eosinophilia)
diseases: Often asymptomatic; intestinal/biliary obstruction; Loeffler pneumonitis during larval lung phase
treatment: Albendazole or mebendazole
pearl: Knobby eggs; fecal-oral; can obstruct bowel/bile duct; larvae migrate through lungs and are reswallowed.

name: Strongyloides stercoralis
morphology: Rhabditiform LARVAE in stool (not eggs); autoinfection cycle; high eosinophilia
diseases: Pruritus, diarrhea; disseminated hyperinfection in immunosuppressed (steroids, HTLV-1)
treatment: Ivermectin (drug of choice); albendazole alternative
pearl: Larvae in stool + autoinfection -> chronic decades-long infection; steroids/HTLV-1 trigger lethal hyperinfection.

name: Taenia solium
morphology: Scolex with 4 suckers + rostellum with hooklets (armed); gravid proglottids; eggs in stool
diseases: Intestinal taeniasis (eat undercooked pork w/ cysticerci). Cysticercosis/neurocysticercosis (ingest eggs)
treatment: Praziquantel (intestinal taeniasis); albendazole + steroids for neurocysticercosis
pearl: Pork tapeworm; ingesting EGGS -> cysticercosis (brain cysts, seizures); eating CYSTICERCI in pork -> intestinal worm.

name: Diphyllobothrium latum
morphology: Longest tapeworm; operculated eggs; scolex with 2 grooves (bothria); proglottids wider than long
diseases: Vitamin B12 deficiency -> megaloblastic (macrocytic) anemia; intestinal infection
treatment: Praziquantel
pearl: Fish tapeworm (raw/undercooked freshwater fish); competes for B12 -> macrocytic anemia.

name: Echinococcus granulosus
morphology: Hydatid cysts with daughter cysts + hydatid sand on imaging; eggs from dog feces
diseases: Hydatid cyst disease (liver/lung); cyst rupture -> anaphylaxis & seeding
treatment: Albendazole + PAIR (puncture-aspiration-injection-reaspiration) or careful surgery; inject scolicidal (hypertonic saline) before removal
pearl: Dogs (definitive) + sheep (intermediate); eggshell-calcified liver cyst; don't spill cyst (anaphylaxis).

name: Schistosoma (mansoni/japonicum/haematobium)
morphology: Eggs by spine: mansoni lateral spine, haematobium terminal spine, japonicum small/rudimentary lateral knob; snail intermediate host
diseases: mansoni/japonicum: portal HTN, hepatosplenic ('pipe-stem') fibrosis. haematobium: hematuria, bladder squamous cell carcinoma
treatment: Praziquantel
pearl: Cercariae penetrate skin in fresh water (snails); S. haematobium -> bladder squamous cell cancer; 'pipe-stem' fibrosis is from mansoni/japonicum.
